---
title: "Hands-on Exercises"
subtitle: "DMgtAsia · Session-IV"
toc: true
---

Practise the workflow from Sessions I to III on a real dataset, in RStudio. Work through the
tasks; each has a collapsible solution. The full scripts are in the repo:
[`exercises/d1_low_birth_weight_demo.R`](exercises/d1_low_birth_weight_demo.R) and
[`exercises/d1_low_birth_weight_solutions.R`](exercises/d1_low_birth_weight_solutions.R).

## The dataset

We use **`birthwt`** from the `MASS` package: 189 births from a study of risk factors for low
birth weight. Key variables: `low` (birth weight under 2.5 kg, yes/no), `age` (mother's age),
`lwt` (mother's weight), `smoke` (smoking during pregnancy), `bwt` (birth weight in grams).

## 1. Load packages and data

```r
if (!requireNamespace("pacman", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(MASS, tidyverse, skimr, janitor, here)

lbw <- as_tibble(MASS::birthwt)
```

## 2. Tidy the column names

::: {.callout-note}
The `birthwt` names are terse. Make them tidy and consistent.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution
```r
lbw <- lbw |> janitor::clean_names()
names(lbw)
```
:::

## 3. Take a first look

::: {.callout-note}
How many rows and columns? What types are the variables? Any missing values?
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution
```r
glimpse(lbw)
skimr::skim(lbw)
```
:::

## 4. Recode for readability

::: {.callout-note}
Turn `low` and `smoke` from 0/1 into meaningful labels.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution
```r
lbw <- lbw |>
  mutate(
    low_birth_weight = factor(low, levels = c(0, 1),
                              labels = c("Normal", "Low")),
    smoking          = factor(smoke, levels = c(0, 1),
                              labels = c("Non-smoker", "Smoker"))
  )
```
:::

## 5. Summarise

::: {.callout-note}
What is the mean birth weight by smoking status? How many low-birth-weight babies by smoking
status?
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution
```r
lbw |>
  group_by(smoking) |>
  summarise(
    n         = n(),
    mean_bwt  = mean(bwt),
    pct_low   = mean(low == 1) * 100
  )
```
:::

## 6. Visualise

::: {.callout-note}
Plot the distribution of birth weight, and compare it by smoking status.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution
```r
ggplot(lbw, aes(bwt)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20) +
  labs(x = "Birth weight (g)", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(lbw, aes(smoking, bwt)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Birth weight (g)") +
  theme_minimal()
```
:::

## Stretch tasks

-   Does mean birth weight differ by mother's smoking after grouping by `race`?
-   Make a clean summary table with `gtsummary::tbl_summary()`.
-   Save your cleaned data to `data/processed/lbw_clean.csv` with `readr::write_csv()` and
    `here()`.

When you are done, you are ready for the **Spatial Epidemiology** module.
